text:
Once, in a quiet town bathed in golden sunlight, there lived a peculiar old owl named Oliver. Oliver didn't fly out to hunt for mice like all the other owls. Instead, he sat all day by the ancient oak tree whispering secrets to the fluttering butterflies.

Oliver had an unusual passion for collecting antique typewriters. He'd pore over old manuals, learning every quirky keystroke. One day, a young girl named Amelia wandered into town late one afternoon. She was searching for an antique typewriter for her grandmother’s birthday.

"Hello there," Oliver greeted her with a soft, squawk. "Care for a story?"

"You would tell me about typewriters!" Amelia squealed excitedly.

"Ah yes," Oliver began, settling into his favorite branch. "It was a rainy Saturday, quite unlike the glorious day we had just a fortnight ago. I was perched above the dreary little town, scanning the newspapers."
